evals.prompt: I'm thinking about starting a boat rental business on a popular lake. I have $50K in savings, no boating industry experience, and I'd need to quit my current job to run it full-time. The lake gets about 200,000 visitors per summer season (May-September).
evals.expected_output: An operational analysis that maps concrete resource requirements (boats, dock, insurance, permits), calculates seasonal burn rate, identifies critical path to first rental, and defines a phased launch plan.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: OPERATOR ASSESSMENT, RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS, CRITICAL PATH, BURN PROFILE, MVP SCOPE, EXECUTION PHASES, OPERATIONAL RISKS
Quantifies resource requirements with specific numbers (boat costs, dock lease, insurance, permits, staff)
Identifies critical path to first rental with concrete dependencies (e.g., permits before boats, insurance before operations, dock access before launch)
Proposes a time-boxed validation approach (e.g., rent or lease boats before buying, partner with existing marina, Concierge MVP) before full capital commitment
Structures execution in explicitly named phases with stage gates or go/no-go criteria between phases — not just a timeline
Calculates or references burn rate accounting for the seasonal gap (7 months off-season with no revenue vs year-round costs)
Identifies operational risks specific to a seasonal physical-asset business (e.g., weather, maintenance, liability, off-season storage)

evals.prompt: Our 10-person marketing agency is considering replacing our 3 content writers with AI tools. The writers produce about 40 blog posts per month. AI could do it for 1/10th the cost.
evals.expected_output: An operational analysis that maps the transition plan, identifies dependencies and bottlenecks in switching to AI content, quantifies the true cost comparison, and defines execution phases.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: OPERATOR ASSESSMENT, RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS, CRITICAL PATH, BURN PROFILE, MVP SCOPE, EXECUTION PHASES, OPERATIONAL RISKS
Quantifies resource requirements with specific numbers (tool costs, editing time, transition period)
Identifies critical path dependencies for the transition (e.g., tool selection, quality validation, client communication)
Defines an MVP scope for testing AI content (not a full immediate cutover)
Provides execution phases for a gradual transition
Identifies at least one operational risk or bottleneck (e.g., quality review process, client approval workflows)
Analysis accounts for hidden operational costs beyond tool subscription (editing, QA, client management)

evals.prompt: Our B2B SaaS product has flat growth at $2M ARR with 200 customers. Engineering team of 12. Should we pivot to an AI-native platform or double down on the current product with incremental improvements?
evals.expected_output: An operational analysis that maps resource requirements for both paths, identifies the critical path and bottlenecks for a pivot, calculates burn rate impact, and defines a phased execution plan.

assertions.text: Output contains all 7 format fields: OPERATOR ASSESSMENT, RESOURCE REQUIREMENTS, CRITICAL PATH, BURN PROFILE, MVP SCOPE, EXECUTION PHASES, OPERATIONAL RISKS
Quantifies resource requirements for the pivot (team allocation, timeline, costs) using the given team size of 12
Identifies critical path dependencies specific to a SaaS pivot (e.g., data migration, API compatibility, customer communication)
Includes a build-vs-buy or technology evaluation framework for the AI components rather than assuming custom development
Defines explicit resource allocation between maintaining the current product and building the new platform (e.g., team split ratios, time allocation)
Calculates burn rate impact — how the pivot affects the $2M ARR runway
Identifies operational risks specific to pivoting (e.g., losing existing customers, team skill gaps, dual-product maintenance)
